summary: Tested 5 bug fixes: celebrate images, inactive service filter, hard delete, wrongly-classified products removal, and Mira's picks filtering. Found and fixed a critical image priority bug in service_box_routes.py. All 15 backend tests pass.

critical.issue: Image priority order was wrong: code used `image_url OR watercolor_image` but image_url had old dead static.prod-images URLs while watercolor_image had correct Cloudinary URLs. This caused celebrate services to show broken images instead of Cloudinary watercolor images. FIXED: reversed priority to `watercolor_image OR image_url OR image` in service_box_routes.py lines 172 and 199.

minor.issue: 21 of 43 active celebrate services still have old static.prod-images.emergentagent.com URLs and NO watercolor_image set. These services genuinely don't have Cloudinary alternatives yet - they need AI image generation or manual upload.

action_items: 21 celebrate services (and many other pillar services) still have old dead static.prod-images.emergentagent.com URLs with no Cloudinary alternative. These need AI image generation via /api/service-box/generate-all-images?pillar=celebrate or manual upload. Consider batch-generating images for all services without watercolor_image.
The `image_url or watercolor_image` priority bug is FIXED in service_box_routes.py. Backend hot-reload should apply the fix automatically.
critical_code_review_comments: FIXED: service_box_routes.py lines 172-176 and 199-202: Changed priority from `image_url > watercolor_image > image` to `watercolor_image > image_url > image` to correctly return Cloudinary images over old static URLs
21/43 celebrate services + 165+ services across other pillars have no watercolor_image and old dead static.prod-images URLs - these need bulk AI image generation
ServiceBox admin shows 1094 total services (703 active, 391 inactive) - the admin correctly shows all services including inactive ones

success_rate.frontend: 90% (services page loading, inactive filter working, celebrate pillar accessible)

test_results.celebrate_cloudinary_images: PASS - After fix: 20/43 celebrate services show Cloudinary images (was 4/43 before fix)
test_results.inactive_filter: PASS - is_active=true filter correctly excludes 391 inactive services
test_results.hard_delete: PASS - DELETE endpoint permanently removes service, returns 404 on subsequent GET
test_results.wrongly_classified_products: PASS - Snuffle Mat, Airtight Travel Food Container, Gentle Oatmeal Shampoo NOT in services
test_results.mira_picks_active_filter: PASS - mira_score_engine.py line 232 has is_active filter

seed_data_creation: Created and deleted TEST-DELETE-SVC-001 and TEST-LIST-VERIFY-SVC during testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Key bugs fixed this iteration: (1) Image priority bug in service_box_routes.py - now prefers watercolor_image (Cloudinary) over image_url (old static URLs). All 5 reported fixes verified as working. 21 celebrate services + many others still lack Cloudinary images (no watercolor_image field) - these need AI image generation. Admin Service Box shows all 1094 services correctly.
rca_of_issue: Root cause of celebrate watercolor images bug: The DB migration (copy watercolor_image -> image_url) was only partially executed OR never executed for many services. Services had watercolor_image = Cloudinary URL but image_url = old static URL. The list_services API used `image_url OR watercolor_image` priority which returned the OLD URL first. Fix: reversed priority to `watercolor_image OR image_url OR image` so Cloudinary always takes precedence.
